race.name: Wicklow 200
race.tagline: Ireland's premier cycling sportive through the breathtaking Wicklow Mountains

climate.overview: June in County Wicklow typically features mild temperatures with variable conditions. Mountain sections are exposed to wind and potential rain.

terrain.overview: Challenging road cycling through the Wicklow Mountains featuring iconic climbs on open public roads. The route combines scenic valley sections with sustained mountain ascents.

adventure.explanation: The Wicklow 200 delivers thrilling adventure through Ireland's Garden County, tackling iconic climbs like Wicklow Gap and Slieve Maan amid stunning terrain, though not quite at the pinnacle of extreme challenges.[1][2]

altitude.explanation: Reaching moderate high points on renowned Wicklow climbs such as the Sallygap and Drumgoff, the route offers solid altitude exposure without extreme peaks.[1][6]

climate.explanation: June timing brings typical Irish weather—mild but potentially wet and windy, as noted in rider feedback, adding challenge without guaranteed perfection.[2]

community.explanation: Attracting up to 3000 riders annually from Ireland and abroad, plus a strong organizing body like the IVCA, it fosters a vibrant cycling community spirit.[1][2]

elevation.explanation: Packed with many of Wicklow's most challenging hills including Wicklow Gap and Shay Elliott, providing substantial cumulative elevation gain for strong climbers.[1][2]

expenses.explanation: Entry fee of €85 for the 200km sportive represents fair value for a premier event, though additional travel and gear costs temper overall affordability.[3]

experience.explanation: Since 1982, this revered Gran Fondo offers riders of all levels a memorable test of endurance on beautiful, demanding roads with chip timing.[1][2]

field_depth.explanation: Draws a large international field of up to 3000, but as a non-competitive sportive, the depth of elite racers remains moderate.[1][2]

length.explanation: The marquee 200km distance hits a sweet spot for epic endurance sportives, perfectly matching the event's namesake challenge.[1][3]

logistics.explanation: Impeccable organization shines through with fantastic food stops, superb marshals, and smooth electronic timing, earning high rider praise.[1][2]

prestige.explanation: As Ireland's longest-running and premier sportive since 1982, with historic climbs featured in the Tour de France, it holds significant status.[1][2]

race_quality.explanation: High-quality sportive execution with scenic routes, well-supported aid stations, and a proven track record make for an outstanding non-competitive experience.[2]

technicality.explanation: Primarily road-based with hilly terrain, the route demands fitness over advanced bike-handling skills on its paved Wicklow paths.[1][6]

value.explanation: €85 delivers exceptional value for a world-revered 200km challenge with top organization, iconic scenery, and lasting prestige.[2][3]

biased_opinion.overview: The Wicklow 200 represents one of Europe's most prestigious and well-organised cycling sportives. The 2025 venue relocation to Russborough House and complete route overhaul inject fresh challenge into a 42-year tradition. While the 3,054m elevation gain demands serious fitness, the impeccable organisation, iconic climbs, and stunning scenery justify its status as Ireland's premier cycling challenge. The parallel 100km option democratises access without compromising difficulty.

final_verdict.one_liner: A world-class cycling sportive combining legendary Irish climbs, exceptional organisation, and unmatched prestige in a challenging 200km package.
final_verdict.full_verdict: The Wicklow 200 stands as Ireland's most revered cycling event and a genuine bucket-list ride for serious cyclists. Established in 1982 and now in its 42nd edition, it has earned recognition as one of the world's most challenging and respected annual cycling events. The 2025 relocation to Russborough House and complete route redesign modernise the experience while maintaining the signature difficulty. With 3,054m of elevation gain across 200km, including iconic climbs like Sally Gap, Slieve Mann, and Wicklow Gap, the route demands serious preparation. However, the exceptional volunteer organisation, comprehensive support infrastructure, food stops, mechanical assistance, and professional finish experience elevate this beyond a mere endurance test into a complete cycling celebration. The €85 entry fee represents excellent value for the calibre of organisation. The parallel 100km option provides an accessible alternative without sacrificing challenge. Cyclists seeking a prestigious, well-organised, scenically stunning, and genuinely difficult sportive will find the Wicklow 200 delivers on all fronts.

logistics.overview: The Wicklow 200 operates from Russborough House in Blessington, West Wicklow, with comprehensive support including parking, changing facilities, showers, and food stops. The event attracts international participants and operates on open public roads with volunteer marshalling and mechanical support.

airports.name: Dublin Airport
airports.notes: Primary international gateway; approximately 1 hour drive to Russborough House

logistics.accommodation: County Wicklow offers extensive accommodation options ranging from hotels to guesthouses. Blessington and surrounding villages provide convenient bases. Camping and camper parking available at event venue.
logistics.registration_notes: Entries open online at wicklow200.ie. €85 for both 200km and 100km options. Event held annually in June. Wristband system for food stop access. Finishers receive commemorative medal.

history.overview: The Wicklow 200 was established in 1982 as Ireland's premier cycling sportive, organised by the Irish Veteran Cyclists Association (IVCA) and now managed by Elite Event Management with IVCA support. The event has grown to become one of the world's most respected annual cycling challenges, attracting participants from across Ireland and internationally.
notable_moments: 1982: Event established as Ireland's premier cycling sportive
2025: Major venue relocation from Bray Emmets GAA to Russborough House with complete route redesign
2026: 42nd edition scheduled for June 7, featuring new course layout with figure-of-eight design crossing at Laragh

course_description.character: A challenging loop through the Wicklow Mountains featuring iconic climbs, scenic reservoir sections, and mountain passes. The route combines gentle rolling sections along Poulaphouca Reservoir with sustained mountain ascents. The 2025 redesign introduced a figure-of-eight layout crossing at Laragh with reversed direction on Sally Gap, presenting fresh challenge to returning riders.

suffering_zones.label: Sally Gap (early climb, reversed direction from previous years)
Slieve Mann and Shay Elliott double-header climbs (traditional signature challenges)
Second half exposed mountain sections (weather exposure)

course_description.signature_challenge: The combination of Sally Gap, Slieve Mann, Shay Elliott, and Wicklow Gap climbs across 3,054m of elevation gain, with the Sally Gap direction reversal and new roads providing fresh difficulty even for experienced riders.
course_description.ridewithgps_name: RideWithGPS records 196.4km with 3,054m climbing for current route

distance_options.name: Wicklow 200
distance_options.description: Full challenge route featuring all major climbs

distance_options.name: Wicklow 100
distance_options.description: Shorter option covering first loop, includes Sally Gap and Wicklow Gap but omits Slieve Mann and Shay Elliott

support_infrastructure: Showers and changing facilities at finish
guide_variables.field_size_estimate: Several hundred participants (exact numbers not published; attracts cyclists from Ireland and internationally)

non_negotiables: Road bike or gravel bike with drop bars recommended
Serious fitness required: 3,054m elevation over 200km demands sustained climbing ability
Weather preparedness: June mountain conditions variable; wind and rain exposure on exposed sections
Mechanical competency: Carry spare tube, multi-tool, pump; mechanical support available but self-sufficiency valued
Early start commitment: 7:00 AM start with 9:20 AM Sally Gap cut-off requires disciplined pacing

citations.title: Entries open for Ireland's biggest sportive
Wicklow 200 set to open summer of cycling with iconic Irish climbs

research_metadata.data_completeness: High for core event details; limited data on historical records, exact participant numbers, and detailed course GPS coordinates
research_metadata.confidence_level: High for 2026 edition details; moderate for historical context and comparative analysis
information_gaps: Exact GPS coordinates for start/finish and major climbs
Historical participant numbers and completion rates
Exact surface conditions and road quality
research_metadata.primary_source: Official Wicklow 200 website and Eventmaster registration platform

videos.title: Wicklow 200/100 2016 - Full Video
videos.description: Did the Wicklow 100 last June 12, 2016. Video taken using Garmin Virb XE(Front) and Cycliq Fly6(Rear). Video is 2.5 hours long.

videos.title: Wicklow 200 6-12-22 | #jmondytv #psi #pinoymtb #W200 #mahinangnilalang #sakalam
videos.description: Wicklow 200 is Ireland's longest running mass participation bike ride or ‘sportive’. The event, also termed a ‘Gran Fondo’, has been promoted annually since 1982 by the Irish Veteran Cyclists Association and attracts up to 3000 riders annually. #jmondytv #psi

videos.title: Track test - GRAN FONDO PRO 2020
videos.description:
Gran Fondo Pro 2020 track test

After weeks of planning and thinking, Starskycycling team decided to host a first and one of its kind, Gran Fondo Pro 2020 in Co. Wicklow. The track will be very tough, tougher than its hilly 200k counterpart, but full of stunning views and adrenaline jumps. Today our team went on a trial spin on the majority of the track to test it and tweak some details. Over the next few weeks a final route will be set and all the event planning will be set in motion. Get yourself ready for 30th June 2020. It'll hurt. But it will be a day you and your family will never forget ☝️

videos.title: Wicklow mountain cycling 7.09.2014
videos.description: Awesome morning cycling up to the mountain with breakfast more than 400m altitude and go down with 60km/h

key_challenges.name: Sally Gap
key_challenges.description: Part of the main 120k route heading from Naas through the challenging mountain terrain.

key_challenges.name: Lugala to Roundwood section
key_challenges.description: Key section on the main route before descending into Laragh for the feed stop.

key_challenges.name: Shay Elliott climb
key_challenges.description: Significant climb after Laragh feed stop, leading toward Sleave Man and the descent into Djouce.

terrain_notes.text: The 50k route around the lakes through Blessington Valley and Naas Valley Mount is described as a short route but very testing, with many experienced cyclists finding it challenging.

race_day_tips.text: There is a feed stop with tea available in Laragh, which comes after the Sally Gap and Lugala sections but before the major climbs.

additional_quotes.text: The main route is 120k starting in Naas, we head for Sally Gap, Lugala, Roundwood, down into Laragh have a cup of tea in Laragh and then after that we go the Shay Elliot, Sleave Man down into Djouce and cross home.

rider_intel.search_text: The Wicklow 200 features a demanding 120k main route starting in Naas and tackling iconic Wicklow Mountains terrain. Riders face Sally Gap, Lugala, and Roundwood before descending to a feed stop in Laragh. The route then climbs the challenging Shay Elliott and Sleave Man before descending into Djouce and returning home. A shorter 50k option loops around lakes through Blessington Valley and Naas Valley Mount, described as testing terrain that challenges even experienced cyclists. The family-friendly 15k route follows scenic canal paths. The event accommodates riders of all abilities with multiple distance options, though even the shorter routes are noted for their challenging nature in the Wicklow Mountains landscape.
